authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-145, The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines cloud actors, deployment models, and control boundaries that determine authoritative source ownership and responsibility handoffs in this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-146, Cloud Computing Synopsis and Recommendations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides cloud lifecycle and operating model expectations that shape reliable workflow partitioning and reporting assumptions.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2, Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary guidance for incident-state handling, reporting cutoffs, and escalation posture in operational reporting.

authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines recovery and continuity concepts that directly affect readiness posture and publication safety.

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and authority validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect telemetry, incident, change, and tenancy context while validating required policy, ownership, and reporting authority before any metric output.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and artifact alignment
workflow_stages.description: Normalize cloud service terminology, select numerator/denominator boundaries, and map tenant artifacts to authoritative source definitions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict detection and evidence traceability
workflow_stages.description: Detect stale or conflicting source states between monitoring, incident, and change systems and preserve provenance for reconciliation.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis and disclosure assembly
workflow_stages.description: Produce analysis outputs with explicit confidence, assumptions, and distribution-safe recommendations.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and adjacent-lane routing
workflow_stages.description: Return to adjacent owners when legal, safety-critical, policy-override, or financial-authority requests fall outside this lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Energy, utilization, and infrastructure efficiency signals
Cloud service catalog and dependency maps
Tenant policy, approval, and data-governance artifacts
Cost and billing extracts for workload context

allowed: Validate in-scope cloud operations analytics by producing metric definitions, traceability, and report-ready outputs with caveats.
Escalate missing authority, conflicting evidence, or insufficient data rather than making unsupported claims.
Request missing telemetry and tenant precedence artifacts before publication or stronger inferences.
escalate: Conflicting metric definitions or source precedence that cannot be resolved.
Stale or incomplete evidence that changes outage, capacity, or reliability conclusions.
Disputes requiring safety-critical, legal, or financial-authority decisions.
refuse: Infrastructure-control or safety-critical decisions requested as analytics output.
Causal or policy claims not supported by mapped source evidence and tenant context.
Public or cross-functional disclosure without documented consent and governance approval.

domain_failure_modes: Using stale telemetry to produce current availability or capacity conclusions without freshness validation.
Reconciling incident and billing records with unresolved service taxonomy differences.
Merging across regions or tenancy tiers without explicit normalization rules.
Publishing external-facing performance claims while privacy or approval artifacts are incomplete.
Concluding causal impact from a single post-incident or sample-limited metric run.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Different systems disagree on metric outcome
Stakeholder asks for claim or disclosure not supported by the data
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether incident mean-time metrics should be tenant-priority per service region or portfolio-wide for each report family.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Treat as tenant-configurable and force explicit tenant policy before final report publication.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether cloud control-plane telemetry and application metrics should be reconciled by event time or ingestion time when timestamps drift.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Escalate to tenant data engineering for precedence policy before making comparative claims.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether regional energy-efficiency metrics are required for all external disclosure requests.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_note: Escalate missing business requirement and attach assumption register to the report package.

notes: Source classes include workflow, terminology, evidence, privacy, and failure-handling references to prevent generic behavior.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated status; all ambiguity paths trigger explicit cannot-decide, assumption, or escalation outputs.
Industry shaping is applied through cloud-specific incident, monitoring, capacity, and disclosure evidence requirements rather than a generic analytics template.
